emails.subject: RE: CA RPS proposal & potential impact
emails.body: Yes, there is a provision for the creation of renewable energy credits, which can be traded. I detailed that in the attachment. Stacey Bolton Environmental Strategies Enron Corp 713-853-9916 direct 713-303-2632 cell sbolton@enron.com <mail

emails.subject: RE: CA RPS proposal & potential impact
emails.body: I under calculated the MW capacity needed to meet the new renewable standard, and am reattaching the analysis. The below table is the portion that was revised. I used a 30% capacity factor since many of the new renewable resources coming on line are wind. The MWs required could be inflated (i.e. cf of 50% v. 30%) in the out years if more biomass facilities are sited. Date<?xml:namespace prefix o ns "urn:schemas-microsoft-com:office:office" RPS Requirement Actual and Forecasted CA Load MWH for RPS MW w/ 30% cf for RPS 6/1/2003 1% 158,315,000 1,583,150 602 1/1/2005 2% 274,772,000 5,495,440 2,091 1/1/2007 5% 289,189,000 14,459,450 5,502 1/1/2009 8% 304,364,000 24,349,120 9,265 1/1/2010 10% 312,246,000 31,224,600 11,882 Stacey Bolton Environmental Strategies Enron Corp 713-853-9916 direct 713-303-2632 cell sbolton@enron.com <mail

emails.subject: RE: CA RPS proposal & potential impact
emails.body: Let's look some more. I don't think there's anything that would disqualify any plants.
